body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
Stablecoin transfers hit a record $1.79T in June as USDC's volume share widened to ~70%; six federal agencies face a July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act rules.
Stablecoin News
Open USD (OUSD) launches with 140+ backers
A new consortium stablecoin backed by Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, Coinbase and Google went live, with Stripe naming it the default stablecoin for Stripe-powered businesses.
GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline looms July 18
Six U.S. federal agencies must publish final implementing rules for the GENIUS Act stablecoin law by July 18, shaping issuer reserve and licensing requirements.
USDC extends lead over USDT in volume race
Circle's USDC captured roughly 70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in H1 2026 versus Tether's ~25%, as June volume hit a record $1.79 trillion.
Stablecoin market cap slips $1.9B in a week
Total stablecoin market cap fell to $311.3B, with Sky Dollar (USDS) leading the decline, down 2.36% to $8.02B amid capital outflows rather than a peg failure.
Hacking & Security Incidents
Summer.fi drained for $6M in flash loan exploit
An attacker manipulated Lazy Summer vault share accounting via a flash loan, briefly spiking displayed APY to 2,080,000% before withdrawing funds and swapping to DAI.
White hats flag $70B systemic flaw in Aptos
Security firm Hexens found a bug in Aptos with a near-90% exploit success rate and attack costs of just hundreds of dollars, patched before any funds were taken.
H1 2026 hack losses hit $1.32B across 344 incidents
Crypto projects lost about $1.32 billion in the first half of 2026; the Drift Protocol hack ($295M) was the largest single incident of the period.
Humanity Protocol loses $30M to key compromise
Attackers stole private keys backed up on a malware-infected developer machine, underscoring a shift toward compromised-credential attacks over smart contract bugs.
